me.pinHintBody: You can also add a PIN.
dailyPrompts: What was difficult for you today?
Who was especially kind to you today?
What have you been thinking about today?
What would you like to experience again today?
What did you learn new today?
What do you wish for tomorrow?
Who did you laugh with today?
What was the best moment today?
What thoughts are on your mind right now?

pin.setupBody: You can protect your diary with a PIN so only you can read it.
pin.forgotHint: Ask a trusted adult to help you reset it.
pin.parentNote: Adults can reset the PIN in settings.
pin.bannerBody: Other people who use this device could read your entries. With a PIN, your diary truly belongs only to you.

s1e01c01_0001.body:
Okay, honestly now.
Am I really the only one who notices how Lisa has changed?

Selfie from Italy.
Then 'Kisses from France'.
New profile pic from the sea. Again 🙄.

I could seriously throw my phone against the wall.

Is it just me??

Nobody writes that it's annoying.
Just: wild, wow, hearts everywhere.
Even Igor. Why Igor of all people.

Am I mad at Lisa 😤?
Or maybe jealous? - No! Not me… Or?

Probably.
And that's even worse.

I want to post a photo like that too.
And then likes. Lots and lots and lots.
So someone says: wow Yasmin.

I wanted to reply.
But deleted it.
Wrote it again.
Deleted it again.

First nice.
Then I wanted to be honest. How arrogant it came across.
I didn't do it.
That's not really who I am.

No idea.
My head is all over the place.

s1e01c08_0002.body:
They really don't understand me.

Is it that hard?
To just find my photo cool.
And like it.

Now I'm the bad guy again.
'Didn't you think about us? Or your parents?'
GRRR.

It was just a photo.
One single photo.
Lisa also swam at the waterfall!
Everyone just said:
'COOL', 'AMAZING', 'WOW'.

Mine was also 'COOL', 'AMAZING', 'WOW'.
But they don't say that.
Instead they send my parents after me.
And who knows who else.

...That was actually really bad.
When I saw Mum... her tears... she was genuinely scared...
For me.
Poor her, I really didn't want that.

But did it have to go that far?? Why do those guys even call home??
It didn't look THAT dangerous.
I thought.
Well.
Maybe it did.
After the rain and everything...

I do feel kind of bad. Sweet of Lukas to have fought so hard for me. And Igor.
Igor.
What must he be thinking?
A closed book.
You never know where you stand.

But he is kind of cool...

Well, Yasmin, pull yourself together! What did you learn?
Ok, I admit it.
From now on I'll always say when I use AI.
In the chat.
And for school too.

I swear.

s1e02c02_0003.body:
What. Was. That. Please.

First Dominik tears me apart in front of everyone.
Then I lose it.
Then Lisa gets in trouble.
Then Chioma gets kicked out.
And now everyone acts like that's normal?

I honestly thought I was quick with words.
'Want me to jog your memory?'
Just a comment.
He was totally overreacting.

And now Lisa acts like I betrayed her.
Sorry, but that 'Yasmin, please!' in the class chat?
Could hardly be less subtle.
If someone put themselves in the spotlight, it wasn't me.

Now she's not the queen for once.
And I'm supposed to be to blame? Pfff.
I see it differently.

Chioma is out.
Lisa is scared she'll be kicked out too.
Well. Welcome to the chat.

And then that fake stuff.
AI fight in the schoolyard.
Everyone laughs. Or pretends to.
And whoever falls for it gets laughed at.

Sounds familiar.
My waterfall photo was enough to make everything escalate.

Maybe that's the real problem here?
Not just Dominik.
Not just me.
Not just Lisa.

This chat...

Everything gets bigger here.
Harsher.
Faster.

Nobody hits pause.

Chioma wanted to hit pause.
And got kicked out.

Maybe being strong isn't about typing the best comeback.
Maybe being strong is more about not shooting back immediately.
But then would I be seen as... weak?

s1e03c02_0005.body:
I'm acting like I don't care.
But I did.

Tom is annoying because he always looks at me like that. Like he understands more than everyone else. Maybe he noticed that I kept looking around to see if Finn would show up at the skatepark. Pretty embarrassing, actually. And then Finn writes stuff like that. Like I'm some pop-up window he can just click away. He doesn't care about anything.
No, not anything. He doesn't care about me.
Just his games.

s1e03c08_0006.body:
Finn messaged me 😊
What an idiot. Such a damn idiot. 😫

What am I supposed to do?

I wanted to be cold. Properly: serves you right.
Almost worked.

And then he brings up the fairground. I think something's actually wrong with him. And he messages me. Of all people!
The lights, the spinning, the chaos. And suddenly I knew what he meant. Those weren't excuses.

The fact that he messaged me was really something.
And I was just angry at him the whole time — for being late, being mean, not showing up. I never stopped to think that maybe he's going through something.

I had seen it actually. But I was too wrapped up in myself.
Because I was angry.

When he asked: "Are you still there?"
I didn't know if he meant in the chat or as a friend.

I hope he messages again.

sections.annoys: What can be annoying about me

myCard.title: My Card
myCard.hint: Here you can design your own card.
myCard.subtitle: Write down what makes you you.

chatrules-chioma-dominik.title: Chat rules: Chioma & Dominik
chatrules-chioma-dominik.description: A quick explanation of why rules help in class chats.

friendbook.mostly: I think before I say something – and would love it if others did that too.
othersLike: I listen.
I stay fair.
I say things that bring order to the chaos.
annoys: I ask too many questions.
I stand up to those who treat others unfairly.
And I don't give up easily.
friendbook.colors: Yellow and orange – warm, not harsh.
friendbook.happy: A hot cocoa with a gripping book. And when it's been worth fighting for something.
friendbook.netRule: Think first, then post. And: no likes for meanness.
friendbook.mistake: I take a breath, honestly say 'Okay, that wasn't good' – and try to fix it.
friendbook.secret: Sometimes I don't understand myself why I always think it's my job to solve other people's problems.
friendbook.funFact: Even in first grade my report card said I was the 'argument stopper'. Unfortunately it's not that easy anymore.

friendbook.mostly:
Calm. Alert. A little windswept. I don't believe in bans, but in understanding.
I surf through data just like through the sea – with balance and a broad view.
othersLike: I don't tell you the answers, I ask questions that move you forward.
With me, learning feels like exploring, not pressure.
I believe that everyone can learn to ride their own wave.
annoys: I ask a lot of questions and don't accept excuses.
I draw out your own thoughts instead of just giving you the answer.
I see a chance to grow in almost everything.
friendbook.happy: When someone realises: 'I decide for myself where I click.'
friendbook.netRule: You can't stop the wave. But you can learn to surf it. 🌊
friendbook.mistake: When I fall, I get up and look at the current again. Falls are part of learning. And next time I'll know what to watch out for.
friendbook.secret: I once helped stop a risky app. Since then I'd rather guide than just watch.
friendbook.funFact: I wear my turquoise streak as a reminder: I always surf on the kids' side. 🦉

friendbook.mostly:
Dynamic. Enthusiastic. Always there when students need me.
I love ideas and new projects.
othersLike: I explain technology in a way that suddenly makes sense.
I take students seriously – even their crazy ideas.
annoys: I say 'This will only take two minutes' – and then it's twenty.
I run over time in class when we're working on something really interesting.
I want to draw out the full potential from everyone.
friendbook.colors: Turquoise. Reminds me of the sea, freedom, and good ideas.
friendbook.happy: When a student discovers during a school project what really motivates them.
friendbook.netRule: Digital media isn't dangerous – but you have to understand how it works.
friendbook.mistake: When I make a mistake, I laugh first – and then explain what we can learn from it. Vale?
friendbook.secret: I firmly believe the key to success is passion. That's why I sometimes take unusual paths in and beyond the classroom – and sometimes don't stick too closely to instructions and rules.
friendbook.funFact: When my tablet crashes, I dramatically say: '¡Traición!' – by now the whole class shouts it in chorus.

friendbook.mostly:
Structured. Alert. Patient.
I believe that order makes many things easier.
othersLike: I notice when someone needs help – without being asked.
I explain things until the students have understood them.
And I forget almost nothing.
annoys: I don't like disorder.
I'd rather ask twice than not at all.
And technology and I... well, we have a difficult relationship.
friendbook.colors: Mustard yellow. Warm, friendly, and not too loud.
friendbook.happy: When a student puts in a lot of effort.
friendbook.netRule: Don't believe everything just because it looks colourful and pretty.
friendbook.mistake: It's usually the wretched technology's fault. And then the students or Mr Alvarez have to rescue me again.
friendbook.secret: I have no interest in understanding technology at all. It's nice that someone always comes to help me right away.
friendbook.funFact: I sometimes mix up proverbs. But as they say: practice makes... well, no master has ever fallen from the sky.

friendbook.mostly: I love attention – and I notice immediately when it's missing.
othersLike: I bring energy.
I always have new ideas.
annoys: I can be quite sulky sometimes.
I have to constantly compare myself to others.
friendbook.colors: Pink. Or silver. Or both – depending on my mood.
friendbook.netRule: When I post something, it shouldn't hurt anyone – otherwise it's not a good clip.
friendbook.mistake: I first say 'I didn't mean it like that' – but if I'm honest, I still have to say sorry afterwards.
friendbook.secret: I edit almost every photo before I post it.
friendbook.funFact: My phone storage is full of drafts I never posted.

othersLike: I push the group forward.
I dare to do things.
When there's action, I'm there.
annoys: I make comments before thinking.
And I realise too late when it's time to stop.
friendbook.netRule: When someone says stop, it's stop. Even if it was 'just a joke'.
friendbook.mistake: I act like it doesn't matter – but actually I need to learn to apologise without making excuses.
friendbook.secret: I can't stand it when someone else wins.
friendbook.funFact: 'Just joking' is my favourite phrase – so I feel like I never have to apologise 😂.

friendbook.mostly:
Calm. Analytical. Helpful.
I like nothing more than sitting at a computer and programming something.
othersLike: I stay objective.
I help without making a big deal of it.
I don't lose my nerves easily.
annoys: I sometimes seem cold even though I don't mean to be.
I want to explain everything logically, even when that's not what anyone wants to hear right now.
friendbook.happy: When the technology works and everything suddenly makes sense.
friendbook.netRule: Before posting, ask yourself: does this help someone – or does it just create more chaos?
friendbook.mistake: When I make a mistake, I first try to figure out why it happened. Then I explain it.
friendbook.secret: That I become uncertain as soon as things are less about facts and more about feelings.
friendbook.funFact: I once asked an AI if I was funny. The answer was: 'Statistically speaking: rarely.'

friendbook.mostly:
Calm. Thoughtful. Observant.
I like to think a few steps ahead before I say something.
othersLike: I stay calm even in stressful situations.
I notice details that others miss.
I listen without judging immediately.
annoys: I explain things too objectively when others actually want to feel something.
I withdraw instead of working through a conflict.
I often assume everything can be solved logically – which unfortunately isn't always true.
friendbook.happy: When a plan comes together or someone says: 'I hadn't even thought of that.'
friendbook.netRule: Just because something is shared often doesn't mean it's true.
friendbook.mistake: When I make a mistake, I first analyse where my thinking went wrong. Sometimes I forget to simply apologise in the process.
friendbook.secret: My motto is: mind over muscle! – But secretly I'd love to be as athletic as Igor.
friendbook.funFact: I sometimes use chess terms when explaining things. Not on purpose. It just happens.

friendbook.mostly:
relaxed, honest
I don't like making a big deal of things and prefer to just be myself
othersLike: I'm pretty easy-going, I think
you can just chill with me
annoys: I often say 'it's fine' even when it's not
I'd rather avoid stress than address it
friendbook.happy: when a song says exactly what I was just thinking
friendbook.netRule: you don't have to look perfect online. being real is enough
friendbook.mistake: I usually act like it's no big deal first. then I overthink it for way too long
friendbook.secret: I sometimes get anxious in situations that seem completely normal to others
friendbook.funFact: I'm always humming some song while skating

articleReader.audioQuestion: Have you listened to the audio?
articleReader.audioHint: You can share your reaction afterwards.
articleReader.doneBody: Well informed. You see what's going on.
